name: Indian Chicken Curry Coca-Cola
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Chicken breasts or thighs (cut into bite-sized pieces)
1 tbsp Curry powder (or more for experienced palates)
1 Cup Coffee cream or undiluted evaporated milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken pieces.
2. In a pot of boiling salted water, cook the chicken with celery tops. Cover and simmer for about 1 hour, or until fork-tender. Drain and strain the broth; reserve.
3. Bone the chicken and cut into 1/2-inch pieces to measure about 2-1/2 cups.
4. In a skillet, melt butter/margarine. Add apple, onion, and curry powder; sauté for 5 minutes, blending well.
5. Stir in raisins, reserved chicken broth (1 cup), and Coca-Cola.
6. Mix flour with coffee cream/either evaporated milk in a bowl until smooth. Add to the onion/apple mixture along with salt and white pepper. Stir and cook over low heat until thick and creamy.
7. Taste for seasoning.
8. Add chicken and transfer to a covered container; chill overnight.
9. Reheat gently in the top of a double boiler over hot water, then serve over cooked rice with a selection of condiments.
NOTE: Provide a sampling of the following condiments for sprinkling on each serving: grated coconut, chopped peanuts, chopped raw onions, raisins, sweet pickle relish, chutney, chow chow, and/or lime wedges.
